rows.notes: Very clear centered year at top of page; no monetary columns present.

rows.description: Computus Dni. Rectoris J. L. Richards a 2d die Novr AD 1850 ad eundem diem revolution AD 1851
rows.notes: Long Latin header across top of page; inscription is legible and no amounts appear on the same line.
Centered section title (spelled 'Arrearagia Recepta' on page). No monetary values on this line.
Subheading under Arrearagia; clear handwriting, no amounts on this line.

rows.description: Gwinear Rectory 1 yr P. Tax deducted
rows.notes: Pounds '58' and shillings '5' are clearly aligned in the pounds and shillings columns. The pence column is blank on the page (interpreted as no pence shown). 'P. Tax deducted' note is written beside description.
Clear three-column numeric entry '19 8 4' aligned under the pounds, shillings and pence rulings. Handwriting for digits is unambiguous.
Reads as '9 14 2' under the three columns. Slight shakiness on one digit (the 9) but placement with the ruling lines makes reading consistent and values validate (shillings <20, pence <12).
Numbers appear clearly as '1 6 8'. Scripting of 'Capons & Brown' is legible; numeric alignment is consistent.

Numeric columns read as '1 16 4' with a small loop after the pence digit interpreted as 'ob' (halfpenny). The fraction mark is faint; I interpret it as a halfpenny (0.5). Moderate uncertainty due to faint fractional symbol.
A single '1' is visible in the pounds column and no shillings/pence are written. Reading of the lone '1' is clear but absence of shillings/pence is assumed (left blank on page).
Name 'Montacute' appears with no monetary figures on the same line. Could be a sub-heading or an entry with no recorded amount.
Place name written larger/left to introduce several tenant lines (Norris, Pickford, Chaplin). No amounts on this line itself, so classified as header.
Three clear digits '9 1 2' aligned under pounds/shillings/pence columns. 'P. Tax deducted' note visible next to description.
Numbers read as '6 9 5' in the three columns; handwriting of digits is distinct.
Appears as '1 12 4' under the numeric columns. 'Chaplin' spelling is slightly cramped but legible and numbers validate.

rows.description: Little Pew Harris late Beeks 1 yr Do
rows.notes: Clearly reads '1 5 3' in the currency columns. Surname 'Beeks' is slightly abbreviated but the numeric columns are unambiguous.

The left-most pounds column for this line is blank and the figures align under the shillings/pence columns as '17 6' (interpreted as 17s 6d). The tenant name is partly faded — read as 'Lynath' or 'Lynam' with moderate uncertainty.
I see a single '10' aligned under the shillings column but pence is not visible and pounds column is blank. The digit is somewhat faded; reading as 10 shillings only is plausible but uncertain.
There is a small '1' visible near the pounds column for this line but the remainder of the numeric area is weak/faded. The place name is partially obscured; assigned low confidence.
A further small '1' is visible (likely a separate small receipt), but ink faint. The description on the left is abbreviated and faint; confidence is low.
Clear centered section title; no amounts on this header line.

rows.description: Killington Rectory 1/2 yr P. Tax ded.
rows.notes: Numeric '10 16 2' is clearly aligned. 'P. Tax ded.' note is legible next to the description.
Pounds/shillings/pence read as '27 3 8' and align with ruling lines. Handwriting of digits is distinct.
Appears as '6 15 11' under the three columns. All digits are legible and validate (shillings 15 <20, pence 11 <12).
Centered section title; the following lines are variable rent entries.

Large pounds value '75' is visible; shillings/pence read as '12 9'. Tenant name next to Killington is partly unclear ('Stonomans' or 'Germans'); numeric columns are reasonably clear but the line is crowded so confidence is moderate.
Reads '13 14 6' aligned under respective currency columns; the 'Do' indicates same locality. Handwriting of numbers is fairly clear.
Numeric sequence '83 7 4' visible and aligns with the column rules. Slight ink blotting on one digit but consistent with neighbouring totals.
Large amount reads '152 11 6'. The hundreds digit is a little compressed but matches alignment of other three-digit pounds entries on the page.
I read '7 4 4' with a faint halfpenny loop after the pence digit (interpreted as ½). The fraction mark is faint and could be absent; therefore moderate-to-low confidence.
Pounds '105' and shillings '15' are visible; pence column appears blank or rubbed. The large pound figure is legible but slightly crowded which reduces confidence.
Numeric cluster reads '19 15 1'. The pence '1' is small but visible. Some ink blotting in the adjacent area introduced moderate uncertainty.
Appears as '4 10 7' under the columns; digits are readable though slightly cramped.
Numeric grouping '7 1 9' is legible; last digit pence '9' is somewhat small but consistent with the column.
Reads as '5 6 4'; slight ink fade on the pence digit but alignment with the columns supports this reading.
Shows '15 6' with pounds and shillings visible; pence column blank. The descriptive text is slightly cramped and the parenthetical '(2 yrs)' is faint but readable.
A repeated '15 6' appears on the following line (possibly a similar item). Text 'late Morrills' is somewhat cramped; numeric reading mirrors the previous line and is moderately confident.

Final boxed total at bottom right reads as '661 18 1' with a fractional marking after the pence that appears to be 3/4 (three farthings). The fraction is somewhat faint so assigned moderate confidence. All columns align to the right rule confirming column boundaries.
